forecasts.reasoning: The recall concerns only 303 lbs of a ready-to-eat wrap with a very short shelf life (already expired by the time of the recall, with 'best by' dates in late February 2024/2025). Because the product has a short shelf life and was distributed to specific vending machines, notification and effectiveness checks should conceptually be very rapid. However, the U.S. Department of Agriculture's Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) has a bureaucratic process for officially changing a recall status from 'Active' to 'Closed', requiring the district office to compile data, submit a closure recommendation, and the national office to approve and update the website. Although 3 months (February 24 to June 1) is a reasonable time to complete the on-the-ground work for a small recall, bureaucratic updates frequently take 6 to 12 months for FSIS to finalize. Therefore, there is a moderate risk the public page will still list it as 'Active' on June 1, 2025. I estimate a 43% probability of closure by the deadline.
